title: Give Every Major Region a Navigable Purpose
summary: Build and verify semantic landmarks in regional heat-response portal audit through prediction, parsed-structure inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain semantic landmarks from purpose and parsed structure.
Apply semantic landmarks while retaining prior HTML requirements.
Diagnose the named misconception in a changed content and interaction scenario.

steps.title: Read the regional heat-response portal audit relationship · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which claim preserves semantic landmarks when the styling and content change? Scenario: regional heat-response portal audit.
steps.why: Give Every Major Region a Navigable Purpose keeps meaning testable instead of depending on appearance. This is checkpoint 4 in regional heat-response portal audit.

content.text: Landmark elements identify major page regions by purpose. Header introduces a page or section; nav groups major navigation; main contains the unique primary content; aside is complementary; article can stand independently; section groups a named theme; and footer carries contextual closing information. A page normally exposes one main landmark and gives repeated navigation regions distinct names. Apply it to regional heat-response portal audit.
